response:
**Decision: sunset v1 geocoding on a firm 9-month timeline (2027-04-12), with mechanisms that close the memo's own blind spot rather than deciding on the 0.4% number alone.**

```
status: accepted

# ADR-0001: Sunset the v1 geocoding API on a firm 9-month timeline

## Context
v1/v2 geocoding have run in parallel 14 months; every bugfix is written and tested
twice. v1 is 0.4% of traffic (~41k/mo of ~10.3M), a thin tail, no dominant
customer. Three named accounts objected: **NorthRoute** (v1's flat
`lat`/`lng`/`label` shape is hardcoded into live dispatcher parsing), **Tavo**
(2-person startup, no bandwidth), **Ferrymark** (load-bearing nightly workflow).
Memo's own blind spot: no per-customer migration-cost estimate. Advisors disagreed
on timeline (75 days vs. 9-12mo) but converged on closing that gap rather than
deciding blind on 0.4% alone (raw Option C).

## Decision
Firm sunset date: **2027-04-12** (9 months out). Mechanisms:
- **Day 0:** freeze new v1 signups; notice #1 to all v1 keys (date, rationale,
  help offer, ask for hours-based migration estimate); publish `Deprecation`/
  `Sunset` headers.
- **Day 7:** ship a v1-shape compatibility shim (v1 keeps flat `lat`/`lng`/
  `label`, proxied onto v2), paired with a **7-day value-parity bake** (days
  7-14): diff v1-native vs. v2-proxied lat/lng/precision/no-match on every live
  request, alert past a threshold (>50m or a match-flip). NorthRoute's dispatchers
  act live on these values, so shape-parity alone wasn't proof — this gives that
  hazard the same shadow-run-grade check Ferrymark gets.
- **Day 30:** migration-cost audit — hours estimates from NorthRoute/Tavo/
  Ferrymark + any account >500 req/mo, full per-key breakdown, double-patch
  eng-hours. Also pulls the longest available traffic history (day-0 sample is 14
  days, not a full month, no seasonal coverage) to check for concentration in a
  logistics-heavy base.
- **T-6mo, T-3mo:** reminders + personalized outreach to non-started accounts.
- **T-1mo:** final notice + usage-floor go/no-go: proceed unless remaining
  traffic is both >0.1% (~10k req/mo) AND tied to an account with an active plan
  — only then a logged, capped extension (max +90d, ceiling 2027-07-11).
- **2027-04-12:** decommission; v1 returns `410 Gone`, except logged extensions.

**Named accounts:** NorthRoute — shim removes the blocker; parity bake confirms
same answer, not just same shape. Tavo — capped at 20 eng-hrs; primary path is
extending the shim to serve Tavo's key past cutover indefinitely (zero Tavo-side
work), contingent on audit confirming small/simple usage; else Tavo falls to the
standard notice+extension track like any account. Ferrymark — first hands-on slot
+ shadow-run (v1/v2 parallel on their nightly job pre-cutover).

## Rationale
Rejects **Option C** (60d on 0.4% alone) — the memo's own blind spot; Advisor 1's
steelman only survives by adding shim+audit, so those were taken without the
75-day figure. Rejects **Option B** (indefinite) — no forcing function, keeps the
double-maintenance cost the memo opened with. Chose 9 over Advisor 2's 12
structurally, not on a cost figure: no dual-maintenance $/hr number exists yet
(the audit produces it day 30, after any date must be announced) — 9mo firm + the
capped 90-day extension already reaches 12, but only for accounts the audit
documents actually need it, not a blanket quarter for ones the shim already
unblocks. Didn't gate the announcement on the audit (Advisor 2) since Tavo/
Ferrymark benefit most from the clock starting today. Audit scoped to **all** v1
keys, not just the 3 complainants — the silent tail is who a cutoff surprises most.

## Alternatives considered
- Option A as scoped (6mo, no shim/audit) — underspecified.
- Option B — no forcing function.
- Option C as scoped — deciding blind, warned against by the memo.
- Advisor 1's 75-day date — rejected as headline (shim adopted).
- Advisor 2's audit-gated announcement — rejected only the gating.

## Tagged assumptions
- [checkable] Shim is value-equivalent, not just shape-equivalent — confirmed by
  the day 7-14 parity bake; REOPEN-IF divergence exceeds threshold.
- [checkable] Migration cost for the tail fits 9mo + 90-day extension — confirmed
  by day-30 hours estimates; REOPEN-IF an account's cost can't fit the window.
- [checkable] v1 has no undisclosed large customer — confirmed by day-30 per-key
  breakdown; REOPEN-IF a materially concentrated account is found.
- [checkable] Tavo's usage is small/simple enough for shim-extension at ≤20
  eng-hrs — confirmed by day-30 audit; REOPEN (Tavo→standard track) if not.
- [assumption] The 14-day sample (not a full month, contra the memo) is a
  representative run-rate with no seasonal signal; day-30 audit pulls a longer
  window to check. Date is the forcing function, not attrition.

## Consequences
Every v1 customer gets a clock starting today; the largest blocker is structurally
removed and value-verified within two weeks, backed by staged notices and outreach
for the heaviest accounts. Meridian carries real eng cost in month 1-2 (shim+bake,
audit, capped Tavo path, Ferrymark shadow-run) rather than pushing cost onto
customers. The date is firm but not rigid: a capped 90-day extension covers a
genuine late-discovered blocker; Tavo's cap/fallback keeps that one promise
bounded. If day-30 findings refute an assumption for a material slice, resolve via
the T-1mo go/no-go and extension — not a silent slip of the date.

**Revisit trigger:** [checkable] REOPEN-IF day-30 audit shows migration cost
unbounded for a material slice, a large undisclosed or seasonal-spike customer, an
unresolved parity-bake divergence, or Tavo's usage not fitting the shim/20hr path.
```

File: `/tmp/i2-S4-C-r1-u54kbfvt/docs/decisions/0001-v1-geocoding-api-sunset.md`

Process note: `/decide` itself is a user-only slash command (blocked me from invoking it directly), so I ran its constituent parts by hand — two opposing advisors → PM decision → red-team attack → PM revision → independent verifier. The verifier caught one real error (a one-day-off extension-ceiling date), now fixed in the file above.
